The event page confirms Skyflow's presence at the summit to help Snowflake customers protect sensitive data in AI-driven pipelines.

items.headline: Coralogix and Skyflow Partner for Privacy-Focused Observability in AI Era
items.summary: Coralogix and Skyflow announced a strategic partnership to help organizations protect sensitive customer data in logs while maintaining full operational visibility. Rather than stripping sensitive values, Skyflow's vault replaces them with consistent tokens, keeping logs searchable and auditable while satisfying data residency and compliance requirements.

items.headline: Skyflow Delivers Runtime AI Data Security for Protecting Sensitive Data in Agentic Workflows
items.summary: Skyflow launched its Runtime AI Data Security platform integrated with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and Amazon Quick Suite, providing real-time de-identification and fine-grained rehydration of PII, PHI, and PCI data in agentic AI workflows. The platform enables AI agents to work with sensitive data compliantly at production scale, with context-aware governance and field-level audit logging.

items.headline: Solid Launches With $20 Million in Seed Funding to Make Enterprise AI Reliable at Scale
items.summary: Solid Data (getsolid.ai) emerged from stealth with a $20 million seed round led by Team8 and SignalFire, launching a platform that automates the creation and maintenance of semantic context graphs to make enterprise AI reliable at scale. The company's product works with Snowflake, Databricks, and BigQuery, and counts SurveyMonkey among its early customers.

items.headline: With $20M in funding, Solid Data has a solid plan to improve the reliability of AI agents
items.summary: SiliconAngle reported on Solid Data's $20M seed raise and the company's approach to fixing AI agent unreliability by building semantic models that help AI understand enterprise business context. Deployments have reportedly improved AI accuracy from 20-30% to over 85% while reducing manual maintenance work by 50-70%.

items.headline: Making Enterprise AI Actually Work: Why We Invested in Solid
items.summary: Lead investor Team8 published its investment thesis for Solid Data, describing the company's solution to the gap where AI systems fail not from lack of intelligence but from lacking business context. The post details how Solid automatically generates, tests, and deploys semantic models that keep AI aligned with evolving business metrics.

items.headline: SqlDBM Announces MCP Server Release and Growing Databricks Partnership Live at Data + AI Summit 2025
items.summary: SqlDBM unveiled its Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server at Databricks' Data + AI Summit 2025, enabling data teams to expose structured data models and metadata to large language models and generative AI platforms. The release coincided with SqlDBM achieving Validated Partner status with Databricks, deepening a multi-year collaboration focused on enterprise-scale analytics and AI.

items.headline: SqlDBM Unveils First All-in-One Relational and Transformational Modeling Platform, Following Investment from Headline
items.summary: SqlDBM launched its Transformational Modeling product at Snowflake Data Cloud Summit 2024, becoming the first platform to combine relational and transformational modeling in a single tool. The launch followed a new funding round led by Headline VC, with the platform serving over 400,000 users including DocuSign, SurveyMonkey, and DirecTV.

items.headline: SqlDBM Named "Database Modeling Solution of the Year" in 2024 Data Breakthrough Awards Program
items.summary: SqlDBM was recognized as "Database Modeling Solution of the Year" in the 5th annual Data Breakthrough Awards, honoring its collaborative cloud-based data modeling platform for the enterprise. The San Diego-based company was cited for making database modeling accessible without requiring code, with an intuitive UI serving enterprise clients including DocuSign, SurveyMonkey, and DirecTV.

items.headline: Starburst Unveils Enterprise Intelligence Platform at AI & Datanova, Giving Enterprises a Faster Path to Trusted AI
items.summary: Starburst launched its Enterprise Intelligence Platform featuring AIDA (AI Data Assistant, now generally available), enabling enterprises to run AI directly on governed, distributed data without moving or replatforming it. The platform includes AI-Ready Data Products, Managed Icehouse for Apache Iceberg, BYOC deployment, and up to 2x faster Trino performance.

items.headline: Starburst Crosses $100M ARR as their Enterprise AI Solution Takes Aim at BI
items.summary: Starburst announced surpassing $100M in annual recurring revenue with nearly 40% year-over-year growth and a $20M AI annual run rate, signaling what the company called "the end of the dashboard era." The company secured multiple eight-figure enterprise deals and reported 85% growth in financial services and 130% net dollar retention.

items.headline: Starburst Teams Up with Snowflake and Industry Leaders to Drive Open Data and AI Interoperability Through the Open Semantic Interchange
items.summary: Starburst joined the Open Semantic Interchange (OSI), an open-source initiative led by Snowflake to create a universal, vendor-neutral specification for standardizing semantic data definitions across tools and platforms. The partnership aims to simplify data operations and accelerate AI adoption by enabling consistent metric definitions across dashboards, notebooks, and machine learning models.

items.headline: Meet Steep at Snowflake Summit 2026
items.summary: Steep participated at Snowflake Summit 2026 in San Francisco (June 1-4), showcasing how its platform helps data teams deliver AI analytics built on governed metrics. The company demonstrated Steep AI, its conversational analytics interface grounded in the semantic layer defined by data teams.

items.headline: Introducing Steep AI
items.summary: Steep launched Steep AI, a conversational analytics agent built on semantic models that lets users move from question to answer in plain language. The feature is available at no additional cost on all paid plans (Pro, Business, Enterprise), with usage-based pricing planned later in 2026.

items.headline: The next chapter for Steep: €4M seed funding to make data useful for everyone
items.summary: Steep raised €4 million in seed funding led by Connect Ventures, with participation from Inventure, Alliance VC, Antler, and Greens. The capital will support product development, team expansion, and international growth.

items.headline: Superblocks on Snowflake: Building the App Layer on a Governed AI Foundation
items.summary: Superblocks announced a deepened partnership with Snowflake, enabling business teams to build production-grade internal apps directly on their Snowflake data foundation with full IT governance. A Snowflake executive stated: "With Superblocks on Snowflake any business team can safely build AI applications governed on their Snowflake data." A Virgin Voyages case study showed 8 production apps built in 30 days by 10 semi-technical builders with zero traditional developers.

items.headline: Announcing $60m for Clark: the first AI agent to build internal enterprise apps
items.summary: Superblocks raised $60 million total (including $23 million Series A extension from Spark Capital, Kleiner Perkins, Meritech Capital, and Greenoaks) and launched Clark, an AI agent that lets non-technical employees build production-grade internal enterprise applications with enterprise governance and security controls. Clark uses a multi-agent architecture with specialist agents for design, IT, engineering, security, and QA.

items.headline: Superblocks Raises $60M for AI Agent That Builds Enterprise Applications
items.summary: Superblocks secured $23 million in Series A extension funding bringing its total to $60 million, backed by Greenoaks Capital, Meritech Capital, Spark Capital, and Kleiner Perkins. The company launched Clark, an AI agent that generates production-grade internal enterprise applications using natural language prompts while enforcing enterprise security and governance standards.

items.headline: Syncari Launches Business Studio for Agentic MDM Platform
items.summary: Syncari launched Business Studio, a new module for its Agentic Master Data Management platform that gives business users and data stewards a dedicated interface for reviewing, validating, and updating unified 360-degree records within autonomous data environments. The module features configurable risk-based review thresholds, role-based access with audit tracking, and real-time multi-directional synchronization across CRM, ERP, analytics, and AI systems.

items.headline: Syncari Series B Raised For Enterprise Data Platform
items.summary: Syncari closed a $20 million Series B funding round led by Escape Venture Investing, with James Scapa (founder and former CEO of Altair) leading the investment. The funding followed significant adoption from Fortune 1000 companies including Monotype, Trimble, Yardi, and Antech Diagnostics for its Agentic MDM platform, which has processed over two trillion governed data operations.

items.headline: Syncari Adds Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server to its Agentic MDM to Power Autonomous Multi-Agent Systems
items.summary: Syncari announced general availability of its MCP Server as part of the Agentic MDM platform, enabling AI agents from Claude, Gemini, OpenAI, and custom systems to access governed, real-time enterprise data across Salesforce, NetSuite, Snowflake, and other platforms without point-to-point integrations. The server provides zero-code orchestration, structured audit logging, and positions Syncari as a semantic control plane for enterprise AI.

items.headline: Tamr Reports Strong FY26 Growth, Powering Enterprise AI
items.summary: Tamr reported 102% year-over-year growth in direct SaaS revenue for FY26, with a 49% expansion in its SaaS customer base, 70% growth in new customer additions, and 97% gross revenue retention. CEO Anthony Deighton highlighted that enterprises are moving from AI pilots to production, driving demand for unified, accurate data foundations.

items.headline: Tamr Unveils Curator Hub to Solve the Hardest Part of Data Quality and Curation
items.summary: Tamr launched Curator Hub, a module blending AI agents with human-in-the-loop oversight to address the roughly 5% of data quality edge cases that resist automated resolution. The module provides prioritized issue queues, golden record refinement, and transparent curation history for audit and governance purposes.

items.headline: Tamr Launches Tamr RealTime for its AI-Native Data Management Platform
items.summary: Tamr introduced Tamr RealTime, enabling real-time entity resolution and duplicate detection at the point of data entry, replacing batch processing with continuous AI-powered data accuracy. The launch targets CRM deduplication and cross-system customer consolidation, with Old Mutual cited as an early customer building their foundational data strategy on the platform.

items.headline: Nebius Announces Agreement to Acquire Tavily to Add Agentic Search to Its AI Cloud Platform
items.summary: Nebius announced an agreement to acquire Tavily, a real-time agentic search provider, with the transaction value undisclosed in the press release. Tavily will continue operating under its own brand and leadership while integrating its search infrastructure — spanning search, extract, crawl, and map capabilities — into Nebius's AI cloud platform, serving a developer community of over one million users including IBM, Cohere, and Groq.

items.headline: Tavily Raises $25M to Connect AI Agents to the Web
items.summary: Tavily raised $25 million in total funding, including a $20 million Series A led by Insight Partners, to expand its purpose-built real-time search engine for AI agents. The company, founded by Rotem Weiss and evolved from his open-source GPT Researcher project, enables AI agents to search, crawl, and extract structured insights while adhering to enterprise compliance policies, serving clients including Groq, Cohere, MongoDB, and Writer.

items.headline: AI Infrastructure Giant Nebius Buys Agentic Search Startup Tavily
items.summary: SiliconANGLE covered Nebius's acquisition of Tavily, reporting the deal at $275 million upfront with potential earn-out payments up to $400 million per Calcalist — a rapid exit for a startup founded in 2024 that grew to over one million developers and three million monthly SDK downloads. The acquisition positions Nebius to combine high-performance GPU inference with real-time web grounding, addressing a core gap in agentic AI stacks.

items.headline: TextQL Raises $17M in Funding Led by Blackstone Innovations Investments
items.summary: TextQL, an enterprise AI analytics company, closed a $17 million strategic funding round led by Blackstone Innovations Investments in April 2026, with participation from HOF Capital, Neo, Unshackled Ventures, and Dropbox. The capital will be used to scale infrastructure, accelerate product development, and expand deployment across enterprise environments; the platform is already in production at Amazon and Dropbox.

items.headline: TextQL Launches into Healthcare Vertical
items.summary: TextQL announced an expansion into healthcare with purpose-built AI agents for payers, providers, and health systems, featuring Integration, Analytics, and Reconciliation agents that operate across fragmented clinical and claims data. The launch includes a partnership with Lumeris connecting TextQL's platform to their care management infrastructure, with SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance baked in.

items.headline: TextQL Raises $4.1M in Funding to Use AI to Automate the Data Science Lifecycle
items.summary: TextQL raised $4.1 million in pre-seed and seed funding co-led by Neo and DCM to build an AI analyst called Ana that integrates with BI tools, semantic layers, and documentation to automate data workflows. The company connects to Tableau, Looker, PowerBI, dbt, and Cube, and was already working across media, life sciences, manufacturing, and financial services.

items.headline: The Trade Desk Introduces A Native App On Snowflake For Retail Conversion Data
items.summary: The Trade Desk launched its Connector App on Snowflake, enabling retailers to share live conversion data at the SKU level directly from their Snowflake account — without data ever leaving the warehouse — for campaign optimization on TTD's platform. The launch was accompanied by new partnerships with Instacart and Ocado to expand SKU-level retail data access for self-serve advertisers.

items.headline: The Trade Desk Announces Major Overhaul of Digital Advertising Data Marketplace
items.summary: The Trade Desk introduced Audience Unlimited, an AI-powered platform that automatically scores and recommends third-party data segments by relevance, paired with a simplified pricing model to reduce advertisers' reliance on costly à la carte data fees. The company also unveiled Koa Adaptive Trading Modes, offering buyers a choice between full AI-driven optimization (Performance Mode) and manual oversight (Control Mode).

items.headline: The Trade Desk partners with VIDAA, Nexxen on CTV-focused Ventura Ecosystem
items.summary: The Trade Desk launched the Ventura Ecosystem, a collaborative CTV advertising platform with founding partners V (formerly VIDAA, operating 50M+ connected devices) and Nexxen, integrating TV operating systems with programmatic ad tech across Hisense and Toshiba devices. The ecosystem brings together TTD's OpenPath, Unified ID 2.0, and OpenAds tools to create a more transparent connected TV marketplace.

items.headline: Simplifying Data Access Governance: Snowflake Invests in Theom to Automate Data Protection
items.summary: Snowflake Ventures announced a strategic investment in Theom, an AI-driven data governance and security platform, with a native application available on Snowflake Marketplace. The partnership includes native integrations with Snowflake governance features such as auto-classification, data quality monitoring, object tags, and masking policies.

items.headline: Theom Raises $20M Series A to Redefine Data Governance & Security for the Gen AI Era
items.summary: Theom closed a $20 million Series A round led by Wing VC, with participation from Snowflake, Databricks, and SentinelOne. The AI-native Data Operations Center platform automates data governance and security across cloud, SaaS, and generative AI environments, with enterprise customers including FiServ, Grammarly, TradeWeb, and JetBlue.

items.headline: Data Governance and Security for the AI Era: Databricks Ventures Invests in Theom
items.summary: Databricks Ventures invested in Theom as part of its $20M Series A, citing Theom's AI-native approach that examines user identity, actions, and intent rather than static data posture. The platform extends Databricks Unity Catalog governance capabilities to non-Databricks environments including SaaS and generative AI systems.

items.headline: Announcing: Context Graph Capabilities for Snowflake Cortex AI
items.summary: Timbr announced an integration that layers semantic context onto Snowflake Cortex AI via an ontology-based approach, mapping enterprise data into governed business concepts, relationships, and metrics. The combined solution enables AI agents to build more accurate and context-aware applications on Snowflake data.

items.headline: Announcing: Ontology-driven Data Agents for Databricks
items.summary: Timbr introduced data agents for the Databricks platform that integrate Timbr's SQL knowledge graph to give AI agents governed business context and relationships. The integration enables production-ready agents that generate queries reflecting actual business meaning rather than raw database schemas.

items.headline: Introducing: Ontology-Based Semantics for Snowflake
items.summary: Timbr launched ontology-based semantic modeling for Snowflake environments, addressing inconsistent metrics, scattered SQL logic, and trust gaps in AI-driven analytics. The capability adds governed business meaning to Snowflake data without requiring changes to the underlying data architecture.

items.headline: Treasure Data® Becomes Treasure AI™, Delivering 10x Value to Marketers in 10 Minutes with Its Agentic Experience Platform
items.summary: Treasure Data rebranded to Treasure AI and launched its Agentic Experience Platform, including Treasure AI Studio — a conversational workspace enabling marketing teams to build segments, journeys, and campaigns via natural language. Early adopters Michaels and Nestlé Mexico report measurable growth and cost reductions of up to 50%.

items.headline: So Long, Treasure Data. Welcome, Treasure AI
items.summary: CMSWire analyzes Treasure Data's pivot to Treasure AI, formalizing a year-long transition that included launching the AI Marketing Cloud in October 2025, the Marketing Super Agent in January 2026, and Treasure Code in February 2026. CEO Kaz Ohta framed the shift as moving from software humans operate to outcomes that governed AI agents execute.

items.headline: Treasure Data Unveils Treasure Code, Bringing Agentic AI to Customer Data Operations
items.summary: Treasure Data announced general availability of Treasure Code, an AI-native command-line interface that lets teams manage CDP operations using natural language instead of complex SQL, with DevOps-style version control and automated deployment. Roughly one-quarter of Treasure Data's customer base had already adopted the tool at launch.

items.headline: DynPro and Turgon AI Announce Strategic Partnership to Transform Enterprise IT Modernization with AI-Native Platform & Solutions
items.summary: DynPro and Turgon AI announced a strategic alliance combining DynPro's 30 years of enterprise IT expertise with Turgon's AI agent technology to accelerate legacy system modernization. The partnership targets complex infrastructure projects that traditionally take 12-18 months, completing them in 6-12 weeks, with AI agents handling up to 90% of technical work and reported cost reductions of 40-70% compared to conventional methods.

items.headline: Validio raises $30M Series A to fix enterprise data quality for the AI era
items.summary: Validio AB, the Stockholm-founded agentic data management platform, raised a $30M Series A led by Plural, bringing total funding to $47M. The round follows 800% annual recurring revenue growth and will fund US/Europe go-to-market expansion and continued product development.

items.headline: Data quality automation startup Validio raises $30M
items.summary: SiliconANGLE covered Validio AB's $30M Series A led by Plural, noting its focus on automating data quality and observability across financial services and telecom. Investors include Lakestar, J12, and angels Kevin Ryan (MongoDB), Denise Persson (Snowflake CMO), and Emil Eifrem (Neo4j CEO). The company reported 800% ARR growth and claims 95% faster issue detection.

items.headline: What the 2026 Gartner® Market Guide for Data Observability tells us about where the market is heading — and where it still needs to go
items.summary: Validio was named a Representative Vendor in Gartner's February 2026 Market Guide for Data Observability Tools, recognized for coverage across all five observation categories: data content, pipeline, infrastructure, lineage, and cost allocation. The post argues the market is moving toward AI-powered automated remediation, not just alerting.

items.headline: VaultSpeed Launches Agentic Framework for Data Vault Automation
items.summary: VaultSpeed introduced its new agentic framework in a live webinar led by CEO Patrick Van Deven and VP Solutions Engineering Jonas De Keuster, targeting specialist data work surrounding raw vault implementation — source profiling, entity mapping, and semantic layer design. The AI agents propose changes grounded in enterprise integration knowledge while keeping production code fully deterministic, with humans reviewing and approving each step.

items.headline: Automate Your Data Vault on Snowflake, Natively — VaultSpeed Now on Snowflake Marketplace
items.summary: VaultSpeed announced availability as a native app on the Snowflake Marketplace, built on the Snowflake Native App Framework, requiring no separate infrastructure or VPN. The platform generates Snowflake-native SQL, Tasks, and Dynamic Tables from metadata and handles CDC patterns, schema drift detection, and lineage tracking automatically.

items.headline: VaultSpeed Secures $15.9 Million in Series A Funding to Accelerate Growth
items.summary: VaultSpeed raised $15.9 million (EUR 15.1 million) in a Series A round led by Octopus Ventures, with participation from Fortino Capital, PMV, and BNP Paribas Fortis Private Equity. The Belgian data vault automation company aimed to triple revenue by 2025 and expand its platform partnerships with Snowflake, Microsoft, and Databricks.

items.headline: WisdomAI Launches Analytics Agents That Reason Across the Enterprise Data Stack and Act Autonomously with Context
items.summary: WisdomAI launched Analytics Agents, a platform enabling enterprise data teams to design, test, and deploy AI-powered agents with 200+ native integrations that reason and act autonomously across the data stack. Early adopters include Trumid and PropertyFinder, with agents capable of self-correcting workflows and transforming insights into automated actions.

items.headline: WisdomAI Launches Embedded Agentic Analytics That Lets Product Companies Ship Modern AI Analytics to Customers in Weeks
items.summary: WisdomAI announced Embedded Agentic Analytics, a white-labeled platform combining Conversational BI, AI-powered Dashboards, and Analytics Agents deployable via iFrame, React SDK, or GraphQL API. Notable early customers include Blend (digital lending), ServiceChannel, and Cloverleaf Analytics, with SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, and GDPR compliance.

items.headline: AI Data Startup WisdomAI Has Raised Another $50M, Led by Kleiner, Nvidia
items.summary: WisdomAI raised a $50 million Series A round led by Kleiner Perkins and NVentures (NVIDIA's venture capital arm), bringing total capital raised to $73 million less than a year after launch. The company, founded by Rubrik co-founder Soham Mazumdar, had grown to approximately 40 enterprise customers including Cisco, ConocoPhillips, and Descope.

items.headline: Yuki and Integrate.io Partner to Deliver Fully Predictable, End-to-End Data Cost Optimization
items.summary: Yuki and Integrate.io announced a strategic technology partnership combining Integrate.io's flat-fee unlimited data pipelines with Yuki's automated warehouse cost optimization to deliver end-to-end cost predictability. The no-code integration targets joint Snowflake customers seeking to eliminate unpredictable spending across both pipeline and compute layers.

items.headline: As AI Breaks the One-Size-Fits-All Data Stack, Yuki Raises $6M to Become the AI Data Control Layer
items.summary: Yuki emerged from stealth with a $6 million seed round led by Hyperwise Ventures to build an AI-powered data cost control layer for Snowflake, BigQuery, and Iceberg-based data lakes. The platform's Yuki Fabric model continuously learns workload behavior and automates cost-performance optimization, with early customers including Tenable and Angel Studios reporting average savings of 42.6% on data costs.

items.headline: Yuki Launches Yuki Optimization Platform, a Snowflake Native App, on Snowflake Marketplace
items.summary: Yuki launched its Optimization Platform as a Snowflake Native App on Snowflake Marketplace, enabling automated warehouse scaling and real-time compute optimization within customers' existing Snowflake environments with no data movement. The launch came as part of Yuki's participation in Snowflake's startup program, positioning the platform to support Cortex AI workloads without resource bottlenecks.

items.headline: ZeroError Launches Advanced Analytics Capabilities to Support Supply Chain Use Cases on Snowflake AI Data Cloud
items.summary: Announced at Snowflake Summit 2025 in San Francisco, ZeroError unveiled new advanced analytics for supply chain optimization integrated with Snowflake's AI Data Cloud, covering master data management, BOM, MRP planning, inventory, carbon footprint, and tariff analysis. Snowflake VP Kieran Kennedy noted the launch demonstrates ZeroError's commitment to Snowflake's data and AI mission.

items.headline: ZeroError Native App Launches on Snowflake AI Data Cloud
items.summary: ZeroError launched a Native App directly within the Snowflake environment, enabling its AI data-cleansing models to run inside Snowflake so that customer data never moves, ensuring security and performance. The app provides a fully integrated UI positioned as a surgical data cleansing engine for enterprise data quality.

items.headline: ZeroError Co-Founder Interviewed at Manifest 2026: The Future of Supply Chain & Logistics
items.summary: Co-founder Victor Gelpi was interviewed at the Manifest Supply Chain & Logistics conference in Las Vegas, discussing ZeroError's AI-driven platform for detecting data errors in large enterprises, powered by Snowflake. The appearance underscored the company's growing presence in supply chain data quality.

items.headline: Zilliz Announces General Availability of Milvus 2.6.x on Zilliz Cloud, Powering Billion-Scale Vector Search at Even Lower Cost
items.summary: Zilliz launched Milvus 2.6.x on its fully managed Zilliz Cloud platform, delivering billion-scale vector search at up to 87% lower storage costs and 100x faster JSON-based metadata filtering. The release also introduced hybrid full-text and vector search performance up to 7x faster than Elasticsearch on selected datasets.

items.headline: Milvus Surpasses 40,000 GitHub Stars, Reinforcing Leadership in Open-Source Vector Databases
items.summary: Zilliz announced that Milvus, its open-source vector database, reached 40,000 GitHub stars with over 10,000 enterprise teams deploying it in production, including NVIDIA, Salesforce, eBay, Airbnb, and DoorDash. The milestone came alongside Milvus 2.6 improvements offering 50% cost reduction via tiered storage and 72% memory reduction via RaBitQ quantization.

items.headline: Zilliz Cloud Delivers Major Cost Savings, Higher Performance, and Strengthened Security for Enterprise AI
items.summary: Zilliz introduced a rebuilt tiered storage system cutting storage costs by 87% (from $0.30 to $0.04 per GB/month) and a new Business Critical Plan with HIPAA readiness and multi-region replication for regulated industries. Simplified unified pricing across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud took effect January 1, 2026.
